question_text: A cyclist pedaling on a flat road experiences a driving force of 150 N from the pedals. Air resistance provides 80 N of force opposing motion, and rolling resistance provides 40 N of force opposing motion. Is the cyclist accelerating, moving at constant speed, or decelerating? Show your calculation.
correct_answer: Total resistance = 80 N + 40 N = 120 N. Net force = 150 N - 120 N = 30 N forward. The net force is not zero, so the cyclist is accelerating.
explanation: The driving force exceeds total resistance, creating an unbalanced net force that causes acceleration in the forward direction.
